page: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a Cloud GPU server configured with an x86_64 architecture. It features 4 shared vCPUs powered by an Intel Core Processor (Broadwell) running at 2.0 GHz, with 4 physical cores and 1 thread per core. The system is equipped with 20.0 GB of system memory, providing 5.0 GB per core. It includes a bundled 360 GB NVMe SSD for local storage and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 8 GB of dedicated VRAM.

Benchmark results show top-tier performance in low-parameter language model inference. For a small 135M model, prompt processing reaches 9881.81 tokens per second and text generation achieves 465.27 tokens per second. For a medium 7B model, prompt processing performs at 3161.22 tokens per second and text generation at 107.91 tokens per second. Conversely, CPU-bound tasks show average to weak performance. The Passmark CPU score is 7273.24, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is rated poor at 20.35 GB/sec. Database operations and static web serving throughput are also relatively weak, measuring 1916.13 on Passmark and 5.81 GB/sec respectively.

This server offers a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads by utilizing a fractional GPU slice rather than a full dedicated card. This resource density makes it highly suitable for lightweight machine learning inference, small-scale LLM execution, and development environments. However, the shared CPU allocation and weak database performance make it less optimal for heavy multi-threaded CPU processing, high-throughput web serving, or primary database hosting.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a Cloud GPU instance featuring 4 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 20.0 GB of system memory, and a 360 GB NVMe SSD. It integrates a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU providing 8 GB of VRAM. Performance benchmarks reveal top-tier capabilities in LLM inference, with a 7B model achieving 3161.22 tokens per second for prompt processing and 107.91 tokens per second for text generation. However, general compute and database operations are average to weak, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU score of 7273.24 and weak database operation metrics. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for developers needing GPU acceleration without the expense of a full dedicated GPU. It is best suited for lightweight machine learning inference, small-scale model deployment, and AI development workloads rather than heavy database hosting or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 4 shared Intel vCPUs, 20 GB RAM, a 360 GB NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA A40 GPU with 8 GB VRAM, optimized for lightweight LLM inference.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram features 4 shared Intel vCPUs, 20 GB RAM, 360 GB NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA A40 GPU for lightweight AI workloads.
tagline: A fractional GPU instance balancing shared Intel compute with dedicated Ampere VRAM for cost-effective machine learning inference and development.
bullet_points: Features a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 8 GB VRAM
Equipped with 4 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 20 GB RAM
Includes 360 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier prompt processing and text generation for 7B LLMs
Shows weaker performance in database operations and web serving
Best suited for lightweight machine learning inference and AI prototyping
